prevalence.source: IHME GBD (cannabis use disorder) + national survey context
prevalence.trend: increasing with higher use prevalence and perceived lower risk

burden.mortalityRate: Low; indirect risks via accidents and mental health comorbidity

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: DSM-5/ICD-11 cannabis use disorder criteria; assess impairment, withdrawal, and comorbid mental health conditions

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Low to moderate; psychosocial treatment is mainstay

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Often identified within CAPS AD or psychiatric services when comorbidity emerges
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Psychosocial interventions (CBT/motivational), harm reduction, and comorbidity management; no approved pharmacotherapy specific to CUD
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Stigma, limited specialized addiction psychotherapy, and comorbid anxiety/psychosis management needs
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: CAPS AD services and integration with mental health care in some municipalities
